rules.blocking_reason: This is the only operative rights grant surface and must remain authoritative until an explicit author decision is implemented.
Official license legal text is reproduced verbatim for the routed grant and is not itself relicensed by the project.
Repository markers and operating-system metadata are not intended publication or reuse artifacts and should be excluded from releases.
Author commentary is interleaved with third-party titles, descriptions, quotations, or source boundaries and cannot receive a blanket outbound license without review.
Authorship, supplied-source permissions, local-project ownership, quotations, and third-party metadata boundaries require record-level review before any outbound grant.
Historical and generated derivatives may embed earlier rights states, assets, or transformed source material and require version-specific routing.
Local fixtures may be author-created while imported results or reviewer material may carry separate rights; provenance must be checked before lane assignment.
The software-like lane is technically separable, but author ownership, generated code, copied snippets, and inbound contribution rights must be asserted first.
Creation history, fonts, icons, source references, AI-generation terms, and embedded third-party material require asset-level review before licensing.
The prose appears project-authored but ownership, quotations, contributed passages, and imported local-project text require author assertion and review.
Facts may be uncopyrightable, but selection, descriptions, imported fields, and contributed records still require ownership and scope review before a public-domain dedication.
Build and citation configuration is reusable only after ownership, generated-content, and embedded metadata boundaries are reviewed.

default_rule.blocking_reason: No declarative rule classified this path, so it remains all-rights-reserved and quarantined from any prospective split license.

non_claims: A candidate lane is not a license grant, ownership finding, or legal conclusion.
No path is provenance-cleared by this rule-based inventory.
The author and qualified legal reviewer must resolve ownership, quotations, assets, imports, and contribution rights before implementation.
